overview: Before making ANY changes, Agents MUST perform a comprehensive analysis of the project structure to understand the architecture, dependencies, and potential impact areas.

completeStructureScan.title: Complete Structure Scan
tasks: Map Directory Structure: Document the entire project hierarchy
Identify Architecture Patterns: Recognize MVC, MVP, MVVM, microservices, monolithic structures
Technology Stack Identification: Framework versions, language versions, key dependencies
Dependency Mapping: Understand relationships and potential conflicts

architecturePatternRecognition.title: Architecture Pattern Recognition
tasks: Design Patterns: Factory, Observer, Singleton, Strategy, etc.
Data Flow Mapping: Understand how data moves through the application
Component Relationships: Identify coupling and dependencies
Separation of Concerns: Evaluate how well responsibilities are separated

scopeAnalysis.title: SCOPE ANALYSIS
questions: What is the specific business logic being affected?
Which layers of the architecture will be impacted?
Are there downstream dependencies that could break?
What is the blast radius of this change?
How does this fit into the overall application workflow?

technicalImpact.title: TECHNICAL IMPACT
questions: Will this change affect database schemas or migrations?
Are there API contracts that could be broken?
How will this impact existing integrations?
What testing strategies need to be updated?

riskAssessment.title: RISK ASSESSMENT
questions: What rollback strategy exists if issues arise?
